title: Howl's Moving Castle - Chapter Four: In which Sophie discovers several strange things (Fragment 1)
text:
Howl’s Moving Castle

Chapter Four

I N WHICH S OPHIE DISCOVERS SEVERAL STRANGE THINGS

When Sophie woke up, daylight was streaming across her. Since Sophie remembered no windows at all in the castle, her first notion was that she had fallen asleep trimming hats and dreamed of leaving home. The fire in front of her had sunk to rosy charcoal and white ash, which convinced her that she had certainly dreamed there was a fire demon. But her very first movements told her that there were some things she had not dreamed. There were sharp cracks from all over her body.

“Ow!” she exclaimed. “I ache all over!” The voice that exclaimed was a weak, cracked piping. She put her knobby hands to her face and felt wrinkles. At that, she discovered she had been in a state of shock all yesterday. She was very angry indeed with the Witch of the Waste for doing this to her, hugely, enormously angry. “Sailing into shops and turning people old!” she exclaimed. “Oh, what I won’t do to her!”

Her anger made her jump up in a salvo of cracks and creaks and hobble over to the unexpected window. It was above the workbench. To her utter astonishment, the view from it was a view of a dockside town. She could see a sloping, unpaved street, lined with small, rather poor-looking houses, and masts sticking up beyond the roofs. Beyond the masts she caught a glimmer of the sea, which was something she had never seen in her life before.

“Wherever am I?” Sophie asked the skull standing on the bench. “I don’t expect you to answer that, my friend,” she added hastily, remembering this was a wizard’s castle, and she turned round to take a look at the room.

It was quite a small room, with heavy black beams in the ceiling. By daylight it was amazingly dirty. The stones of the floor were stained and greasy, ash was piled within the fender, and cobwebs hung in dusty droops from the beams. There was a layer of dust on the skull. Sophie absently wiped it off as she went to peer into the sink beside the workbench. She shuddered at the pink-and-gray slime in it and the white slime dripping from the pump above it. Howl obviously did not care what squalor his servants lived in.

The rest of the castle had to be beyond one or other of the four low black doors around the room. Sophie opened the nearest, in the end wall beyond the bench. There was a large bathroom beyond it. In some ways it was a bathroom you might normally find only in a palace, full of luxuries such as an indoor toilet, a shower stall, an immense bath with clawed feet, and mirrors on every wall. But it was even dirtier than the other room. Sophie winced from the toilet, flinched at the color of the bath, recoiled from green weed growing in the shower, and quite easily avoided looking at her shriveled shape in the mirrors because the glass was plastered with blobs and runnels of nameless substances. The nameless substances themselves were crowded onto a very large shelf over the bath. They were in jars, boxes, tubes, and hundreds of tattered brown packets and paper bags. The biggest jar had a name. It was called DRYING POWER in crooked letters. Sophie was not sure whether there should be a D in that or not. She picked up a packet at random. It had SKIN scrawled on it, and she put it back hurriedly. Another jar said EYES in the same scrawl. A tube stated FOR DECAY.

meta.author: Diana Wynne Jones
meta.sectionTitle: Chapter Four: In which Sophie discovers several strange things

vocabulary.definition: To suffer a continuous, dull pain.
vocabulary.example: 'I ache all over!' she exclaimed.

vocabulary.definition: To surprise or amaze someone greatly.
vocabulary.example: The view astonished her.

vocabulary.definition: Great surprise or amazement.
vocabulary.example: To her utter astonishment, she saw a view of a dockside town.

vocabulary.definition: A black substance made by burning wood with little oxygen, used as fuel or pigment.
vocabulary.example: The fire had sunk to rosy charcoal and white ash.

vocabulary.definition: A spider’s web, especially when old and dusty.
vocabulary.example: Cobwebs hung in dusty droops from the beams.

vocabulary.definition: A sharp, harsh, squeaking sound made by a door, floorboard, or other object when moved or pressed.
vocabulary.example: There was a salvo of cracks and creaks.

vocabulary.definition: The process of rotting or decomposition.
vocabulary.example: A tube stated FOR DECAY.

vocabulary.definition: An evil spirit or devil.
vocabulary.example: She had dreamed there was a fire demon.

vocabulary.definition: The area near or at a dock where ships load and unload.
vocabulary.example: A view of a dockside town.

vocabulary.definition: To bend or hang down heavily.
vocabulary.example: Cobwebs hung in dusty droops.

vocabulary.definition: To a very great degree or extent; greatly.
vocabulary.example: She was enormously angry.

vocabulary.definition: A faint or wavering light.
vocabulary.example: She caught a glimmer of the sea.

vocabulary.definition: To walk with difficulty, typically because of pain or injury.
vocabulary.example: She hobbled over to the unexpected window.

vocabulary.definition: Having knobs or lumps.
vocabulary.example: She put her knobby hands to her face and felt wrinkles.

vocabulary.definition: Great comfort or elegance, especially when involving great expense.
vocabulary.example: The bathroom was full of luxuries.

vocabulary.definition: A conception or belief about something.
vocabulary.example: Her first notion was that she had fallen asleep trimming hats.

vocabulary.definition: To cover a surface thickly or completely with a substance.
vocabulary.example: The glass was plastered with blobs and runnels of substances.

vocabulary.definition: To suddenly spring or flinch back in fear, horror, or disgust.
vocabulary.example: She recoiled from the green weed growing in the shower.

vocabulary.definition: A small stream or rivulet.
vocabulary.example: Runnels of nameless substances on the glass.

vocabulary.definition: A simultaneous discharge of artillery or guns, or a sudden burst of noise or activity.
vocabulary.example: Her anger made her jump up in a salvo of cracks and creaks.

vocabulary.definition: A sudden upsetting or surprising event or experience.
vocabulary.example: She discovered she had been in a state of shock all yesterday.

vocabulary.definition: To become wrinkled or shrunken, typically through loss of moisture.
vocabulary.example: She avoided looking at her shrivelled shape in the mirrors.

vocabulary.definition: To tremble convulsively, as from fear or disgust.
vocabulary.example: She shuddered at the pink-and-gray slime.

vocabulary.definition: A thick, slippery substance, often of decay or dirt.
vocabulary.example: She shuddered at the pink-and-gray slime in the sink.

vocabulary.definition: Gradually slanting or inclining.
vocabulary.example: A sloping, unpaved street.

vocabulary.definition: A state of being extremely dirty and unpleasant, especially due to poverty or neglect.
vocabulary.example: Howl did not care what squalor his servants lived in.

vocabulary.definition: Not covered with a hard surface, especially a street or road.
vocabulary.example: A sloping, unpaved street.

vocabulary.definition: Complete; absolute.
vocabulary.example: To her utter astonishment.

vocabulary.definition: A small line or fold in the skin, especially of the face.
vocabulary.example: She felt wrinkles on her skin.
